fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 79. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 74.

course_description.character: The race starts and finishes at the 1,850 m Orcières-Merlette ski station, initially descending into the Champsaur before climbing toward Ancelle. It then crosses the Col de Moissière and follows panoramic mountain roads through Beaurepaire and Les Costes. The decisive back half stacks the Chaillol and Serre-Eyraud ascents, including extremely steep ramps, before the final climb back to Orcières-Merlette. It's a paved, climbing-dense Southern Alps route defined by repeated valley-to-resort elevation changes rather than one isolated giant col — four distinct late-race pressure points packed into 134 km. Roads stay open to normal traffic, with only temporary control at selected points.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Serre-Eyraud ascent — 518 m gain, 7.6% average with ramps to 20%, the sharpest advertised wall on the course, arriving in the closing stages before the final climb back to Orcières-Merlette.
suffering_zones: Col de Moissière — summit 1,571 m, 659 m ascent at 5.9% average, 18% maximum
Chaillol — summit 1,540 m, 497 m ascent at 7.3% average, 12% maximum
Serre-Eyraud — summit 1,675 m, 518 m ascent at 7.6% average, ramps to 20%, the sharpest advertised wall
Orcières-Merlette finale — summit 1,850 m, roughly 7.1 km at 6.7% for the full station ascent, with the race's own climb table listing a maximum of 14%

logistics.transport: Driving is simplest; Gap station is approximately 45 minutes away, while the nearest airports require roughly 2.25-3 hours by road.

history.origin_story: Ran as GFNY France-Orcières through 2025, part of the global GFNY World Series. The 2026 edition uses a new name (GF Orcières), a new domain, and 'new event' positioning — a genuine separation/rebrand, not simply a renamed GFNY race, and it is no longer part of the GFNY World Series.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Rebrand, Not the Same Event You Might Expect
biased_opinion.summary: This race ran as GFNY France-Orcières through 2025 as part of the global GFNY World Series; the 2026 edition dropped GFNY entirely, rebranding as GF Orcières on a new domain (gforcieres.com) with its own 'new event' positioning. That's a real separation, not a rename — riders expecting GFNY's competitive age-category format and global series ranking should verify current rules before entering. What hasn't changed is the terrain: 134 km/3,300-3,400 m through the Southern Alps, with four genuine late-race pressure points (Col de Moissière, Chaillol, Serre-Eyraud, and the Orcières-Merlette finish climb) and real Tour de France heritage at the resort itself. The old GFNY domain is still live even though it no longer represents the current event, which is its own source of confusion for anyone searching for it.
strengths: Exceptional climbing density for 134 km, with four distinct late-race pressure points
Strong scenery mix: Champsaur bocage, wooded mountain roads, Écrins panoramas, and a high-altitude ski-station finish
Authentic Tour de France heritage at Orcières-Merlette rather than manufactured branding
Gran, Medio, and Rando formats give riders a choice of distance/intensity
weaknesses: No longer part of the GFNY World Series as of 2026 — riders expecting GFNY's competitive format or global ranking will not find it here
Identity confusion: the old GFNY domain remains online even though it no longer represents the current, independent GF Orcières event
Official materials disagree on total climbing — 3,300 m vs. 3,400 m depending on the source — and parts of the new website still contain placeholders
Roads are open to normal traffic with only temporary control at selected points
Remote logistics: nearest airports are roughly 2.25-3 hours by road; driving is the practical option
biased_opinion.bottom_line: The climbing and Southern Alps scenery are genuinely strong, but this is not the same competitive product it was under GFNY branding — check current entry rules and don't assume GFNY World Series status carries over.

final_verdict.one_liner: Alpine beast with Tour pedigree and Écrins majesty.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes for the climbing density and Écrins scenery. If you specifically want GFNY World Series competitive status, verify current rules first — this event separated from GFNY for 2026.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a GFNY World Series event with a comparable Alpine profile, look at other current GFNY France or Alps chapters.

videos.title: GFNY Orcières. New race in France!
Ambassadeur Orcieres Merlette - Reco GFNY & Nouvelles roues RAR 1090gr
videos.description:
Dans cette vidéo je vous emmène explorer les superbes paysages de la station d'Orcieres et du Champsaur. Des routes empruntés sur la prochaine GFNY Orcieres (27 Juillet) et lors de l'Orcieres Cycling festival

L'occasion de vous présenter mon partenariat et celui du team cyclosportif avec la station d'Orcieres Merlette.

Je vous présente également mes nouvelles roues RAR ! Une nouveauté made in France : 52mm de profil pour moins de 1100g 

Plus d'informations ici : https://www.roues-rar.fr/fr/content/53-aura

videos.curation_reason: Recent course reconnaissance video showing the actual GFNY Orcieres route with good view count and production quality.

eligibility.notes: IDENTITY CORRECTION (2026-07-24): confirmed separated/rebranded from GFNY France-Orcières (ran under GFNY through 2025) to the independent 'GF Orcières' for 2026, no longer part of the GFNY World Series. New official site gforcieres.com. 2026 edition scheduled 23 August; the old orcieres.gfny.com domain is still live but does not represent the current event.
